agency_name: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW)
portfolio: Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water
executive_summary: DCCEEW is a large, multi-mandate policy and regulatory department (~5,790 staff) delivering an exceptionally broad reform agenda spanning the energy transition (net zero by 2050, 82% renewables by 2030, Capacity Investment Scheme), wholesale reform of Australia's environmental laws (EPBC reform, standing up a national Environment Protection Agency), the Murray-Darling Basin Plan and an additional 450 GL water recovery target, Antarctic operations, and the National Climate Risk Assessment/National Adaptation Plan. The plan signals heavy reform delivery pressure, a recent and still-stabilising ICT separation from shared services (first full year as a self-managed ICT provider in 2025-26), and an explicit push to bring core work in-house under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework — which tempers some advisory demand but creates clear scope for specialist delivery, assurance and data work. Numerous new and reworked performance measures, a maturing enterprise risk and integrity agenda, and multiple named cross-jurisdictional programs make this a strong prospect for delivery assurance, performance/evaluation, data and digital, and program-delivery support. The Strategic Commissioning Framework insourcing intent is the key commercial caveat: position around specialist capability, build/implementation and independent assurance rather than generalist staff augmentation.

strategic_priorities.title: Net Zero Plan and sectoral decarbonisation
strategic_priorities.description: Lead development of a new Net Zero Plan underpinned by six sectoral decarbonisation plans, set the 2035 emissions target (next NDC), and lead the Electricity and Energy Sector Plan while supporting other sector plans.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: leading development of a new Net Zero Plan for Australia, underpinned by 6 sectoral decarbonisation plans... including our 2035 emissions reduction target.

strategic_priorities.title: Energy market transformation and investment
strategic_priorities.description: Drive renewable investment via the Capacity Investment Scheme (target uplifted to 40 GW), Renewable Energy Transformation Agreements with states, offshore wind framework, Rewiring the Nation and consumer energy programs while maintaining security, reliability and affordability.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: A further target uplift announced on 29 July 2025 expanded the scheme's target to a total of 40 GW new capacity nationally.

strategic_priorities.title: Environmental law reform and a national EPA
strategic_priorities.description: Reform Australia's environmental laws to streamline assessments and speed up decisions, and progress arrangements to establish Australia's first national Environment Protection Agency.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: During 2025-26, arrangements will be progressed to establish Australia's first national Environment Protection Agency.

strategic_priorities.title: Climate adaptation and resilience
strategic_priorities.description: Release and implement the National Climate Risk Assessment and National Adaptation Plan, including designing a monitoring, evaluation and learning (MEL) system, and run the whole-of-government Climate Risk and Opportunity Management Program.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Design a monitoring, evaluation and learning (MEL) system for the National Adaptation Plan.

strategic_priorities.title: Murray-Darling Basin Plan delivery and water reform
strategic_priorities.description: Deliver the Basin Plan including the additional 450 GL of environmental water by 31 December 2027, conduct the statutory review of the Water Act 2007, finalise a new National Water Agreement, and advance the Aboriginal Water Entitlements Program.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: the department is focused on delivering the Basin Plan, including the additional 450 GL of environmental water by 31 December 2027.

strategic_priorities.title: Nature positive, 30 by 30 and Nature Repair Market
strategic_priorities.description: Protect 30% of land and sea by 2030, implement the $250m Australian Bushland Program, deliver the Threatened Species Action Plan and Sustainable Ocean Plan, and unlock private capital through the Nature Repair Market.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: protect 30% of Australia's land and sea, reform Australia's Environmental laws, and double the circularity of Australia's economy.

strategic_priorities.title: Circular economy transition
strategic_priorities.description: Implement the Circular Economy Framework to double Australia's circularity by 2035, set standards and targeted regulation, and lift national resource recovery toward 80% by 2030.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: implementing the Circular Economy Framework... including doubling Australia's circularity by 2035.

strategic_priorities.title: ICT self-management, data and AI uplift
strategic_priorities.description: Stabilise, secure and rationalise newly self-managed ICT after exiting shared services, migrate off legacy systems to cloud, deliver an AI-enabled digital experience, and mature the data function under the Enterprise Data Strategy.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: In June 2025, the department successfully concluded the 2-year transition journey away from ICT shared services arrangements and is entering its first full year providing its own managed ICT services.

reform_and_change_drivers: Legislated emissions targets (43% by 2030, net zero by 2050) and the obligation to submit a new 2035 NDC under the Paris Agreement.
Water Amendment (Restoring Our Rivers) Act 2023 mandating 450 GL additional environmental water by 31 December 2027 and a statutory review of the Water Act 2007.
Environmental law reform program and creation of a national Environment Protection Agency (machinery-of-government change).
Release of the National Climate Risk Assessment and National Adaptation Plan, plus whole-of-government Climate Risk and Opportunity Management Program with mandatory climate risk reporting.
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework driving insourcing of core functions (Accounting/Finance, ICT, Data/Research, Policy, Communications, HR, Compliance, Science).
Exit from ICT shared services into self-managed ICT, with legacy-system replacement and cloud migration underway.
Capacity Investment Scheme target uplift to 40 GW and rollout of offshore wind, hydrogen and low-carbon liquid fuels frameworks.
Increasing climate hazard severity and economic exposure driving demand for trusted climate risk information.
Significant new and amended PGPA performance measures introduced to reflect a changing operating context.
challenges_and_risks: Enterprise risk that the department cannot develop, deliver and implement effective regulatory practice or influential, integrated policy and programs across an unusually broad mandate.
Attraction, development and retention of staff with the right capabilities (named enterprise risk), compounded by insourcing targets that increase in-house delivery load.
Newly self-managed ICT environment requiring stabilisation, legacy-system replacement and cyber uplift — explicit enterprise risk on security/availability of data and information systems.
Climate-related risks to the department's own operations and achievement of purposes (enterprise risk 5), including APS Net Zero 2030 commitments.
Delivery of the 450 GL water recovery target by a hard statutory deadline (31 Dec 2027) with much depending on Basin states.
Achievement of 30 by 30 and emissions targets depends heavily on states/territories and factors outside the department's direct control.
Integrity and culture pressures evidenced by the Safe to Speak Up campaign and the Independent Review of Workplace Culture and Change at the Australian Antarctic Division.
Many performance measures rely on external data, modelling and case-study/qualitative approaches, creating attribution and evidence challenges.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO maintains a dedicated audit work program for the Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water portfolio, setting out the forward pipeline and completed audits.
anao_findings.implication: A single monitoring point for upcoming audit scopes that often precede demand for remediation, assurance and program support.

anao_findings.summary: Performance audit 'Governance of Climate Change Commitments' (DCCEEW), published around January 2024. Detailed findings not retrieved.
anao_findings.implication: Scrutiny of how climate commitments are governed signals potential demand for delivery-tracking, reporting and independent assurance of the Net Zero Plan and adaptation commitments.

anao_findings.summary: Performance audit 'Corporate Planning in the Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water'. Detailed findings not retrieved.
anao_findings.implication: Corporate-planning audits commonly precede demand for performance-framework and enterprise-governance uplift.

anao_findings.summary: Performance audit 'Community Batteries for Household Solar Program' (DCCEEW). Detailed findings not retrieved.
anao_findings.implication: A delivery audit of a flagship energy grant program is a likely trigger for grants-administration and program-assurance remediation work.

anao_findings.summary: Performance audit 'Implementation of the National Environmental-Economic Accounting Strategy and Action Plan'. Detailed findings not retrieved.
anao_findings.implication: Implementation-focused audit points to scope for delivery, data and reporting advisory in environmental-economic accounting.

parliamentary_matters.summary: The Senate Environment and Communications Legislation/References Committee scrutinises the DCCEEW portfolio, including through Senate Estimates.
parliamentary_matters.implication: Estimates transcripts and inquiry pages are the primary early-warning point for program, funding and performance issues that precede consulting demand.

opportunity_areas.area: Delivery assurance of the Net Zero Plan and sectoral decarbonisation plans
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Lead a new Net Zero Plan underpinned by six sectoral plans and the 2035 NDC, against a backdrop of ANAO scrutiny of climate-commitment governance.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent, gateway-style delivery assurance and milestone health checks across the Net Zero Plan program, with a commitment-tracking and reporting framework — directly responsive to the ANAO 'Governance of Climate Change Commitments' audit.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names the Net Zero Plan and six sectoral plans as a central activity, and the ANAO has audited governance of climate commitments — a strong, time-bound assurance demand.

opportunity_areas.area: National Adaptation Plan MEL system design and evaluation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Design a monitoring, evaluation and learning (MEL) system for the National Adaptation Plan in 2025-26 and assess effectiveness against priority climate risks.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Design of a PGPA-aligned MEL/evaluation framework for the National Adaptation Plan, with indicators tied to National Climate Risk Assessment priorities, plus an evaluation plan for forward years.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Key activity 1.3 and measure CCE08 explicitly require designing a MEL system in 2025-26 and implementing it from 2026-27 — a named, scoped need.

opportunity_areas.area: ICT stabilisation, legacy migration and cloud delivery
opportunity_areas.agency_need: First full year self-managing ICT after exiting shared services; need to secure, stabilise and rationalise networks and migrate off legacy systems to cloud.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and delivery support for legacy-system decommissioning and cloud migration, systems integration and business/process analysis, and service transition/operations support during the stabilisation period.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan states the department concluded its shared-services exit in June 2025 and is focused on securing, stabilising and rationalising ICT and reducing dependency on legacy systems — a clear build/implementation need.

opportunity_areas.area: Cyber security uplift of newly self-managed ICT
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Enhanced cyber security and information management for a freshly self-managed ICT estate, with a standing enterprise risk on data/system security and PSPF/ISM alignment.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Security assessment and assurance of the new ICT environment against PSPF/ISM, including cloud-migration security review and a prioritised cyber-uplift roadmap.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Enterprise risk 7 and the ICT section explicitly cite enhanced cyber security alongside the shared-services exit and cloud migration.

opportunity_areas.area: Enterprise Data Strategy and AI governance maturity
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Mature the data function, embed data governance and analytics in decision-making, manage First Nations data, and govern responsible AI use.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Data governance and information-management uplift under the Enterprise Data Strategy, including analytics capability, AI-governance assurance against the responsible-AI policy, and First Nations data stewardship design.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names the Enterprise Data Strategy, AI Transparency Statement and a commitment to data-driven culture and First Nations data considerations.

opportunity_areas.area: Performance measure and evaluation framework redesign
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Numerous new/amended PGPA measures, several reliant on qualitative case studies and external data with acknowledged attribution difficulty (e.g. CO01, regulator performance).
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A PGPA-aligned performance-measure redesign and regulator-performance measurement framework, strengthening evidence chains and reducing attribution risk — building on the ANAO corporate-planning audit context.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Appendix 2 lists extensive measure changes and the plan states the department is 'continuing to refine our approach to regulatory performance measurement'; an ANAO corporate-planning audit reinforces this.

opportunity_areas.area: 450 GL water recovery program delivery and assurance
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Deliver the additional 450 GL of environmental water by a hard statutory deadline of 31 December 2027, with substantial state and transaction dependencies.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Program/PMO and benefits-tracking support for the 450 GL recovery program, with delivery assurance against the 200/300/450 GL milestones and a risk-managed pathway to the statutory deadline.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Key activity 4.1 and measure WA02 set hard cumulative GL targets to a legislated deadline, with tolerances explicitly referencing 'all reasonable steps' under the Water Act — a clear delivery-pressure signal.

opportunity_areas.area: Stand-up of the national Environment Protection Agency
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Progress arrangements to establish Australia's first national EPA alongside environmental law reform — a machinery-of-government and operating-model challenge.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Target operating model and governance design for the new EPA, including functional split from the department, accountability frameworks and transition planning, with change-management support.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan states arrangements to establish a national EPA will be progressed in 2025-26 — a named structural change generating design and transition demand.

opportunity_areas.area: Grants-program design and assurance for energy/nature programs
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Administer multiple large grant and investment programs (Cheaper Home Batteries, Community Batteries, Nature Repair Market, Clean Energy Futures Grants), with ANAO interest in the Community Batteries program.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Grants design review, probity (non-legal) and program-assurance support for energy and nature grant programs, responsive to the ANAO Community Batteries audit.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names several grant programs and the ANAO has audited the Community Batteries for Household Solar Program — a credible grants-assurance signal.

opportunity_areas.area: Workforce and capability planning under insourcing
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Bring core work in-house under the Strategic Commissioning Framework while managing a standing enterprise risk on attracting and retaining capable staff.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Workforce planning and capability-uplift support to operationalise insourcing targets across ICT, data, finance and policy functions, including capability-framework rollout and targeted recruitment/training design.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to reducing outsourcing across named functions and identifies workforce attraction/retention as enterprise risk 3 — but insourcing intent caps the size of any advisory engagement.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the Climate Change group / Net Zero Plan branch with an independent delivery-assurance and commitment-tracking proposition, explicitly referencing the ANAO 'Governance of Climate Change Commitments' audit.
recommended_actions.rationale: A named flagship program plus a relevant ANAO audit creates clear, time-bound demand for assurance that is less affected by insourcing pressure.

recommended_actions.action: Engage the CIO/CTO and ICT division on cloud-migration delivery, legacy decommissioning and a PSPF/ISM-aligned cyber assessment, timed to the first full year of self-managed ICT.
recommended_actions.rationale: The June 2025 shared-services exit creates an acute, immediate need to stabilise, secure and rationalise ICT — a build/implementation gap the firm can fill where APS cannot insource fast enough.

recommended_actions.action: Offer the Climate Adaptation branch a scoped MEL/evaluation framework engagement for the National Adaptation Plan ahead of the 2025-26 design milestone.
recommended_actions.rationale: Measure CCE08 names MEL system design as a 2025-26 deliverable — a discrete, fundable piece with a clear deadline.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a capability statement on PGPA performance-measure redesign and regulator-performance frameworks for the performance/governance function and CFO area.
recommended_actions.rationale: Extensive measure changes plus an ANAO corporate-planning audit and stated intent to refine regulatory performance measurement indicate latent demand.

recommended_actions.action: Monitor the ANAO portfolio work program and Senate Environment and Communications Committee Estimates for emerging findings on the Net Zero Plan, water recovery, EPBC reform and grant programs; re-run news research on EPBC/Nature Positive, CIS and water buybacks.
recommended_actions.rationale: External research was incomplete; live controversy and audit signals in these areas would sharpen and re-prioritise the pitch.

recommended_actions.action: Open a conversation with the Water Group on PMO/delivery-assurance support for the 450 GL recovery program against its statutory deadline.
recommended_actions.rationale: A hard 31 December 2027 legislated target with state dependencies and 'all reasonable steps' language creates delivery-risk demand.

engagement_targets: Deputy Secretary / Group Head, Climate Change (Net Zero Plan and adaptation)
Chief Information Officer / ICT division (post-shared-services ICT, cloud, cyber)
Chief Risk Officer and the independent Audit and Risk Committee
Chief Data Officer / Enterprise Data Strategy lead and AI governance owner
Performance / corporate-planning and governance branch (PGPA measures)
Water Group / Murray-Darling Basin Plan delivery branch
Environment Group reform team leading the national EPA stand-up and EPBC reform
Chief Operating Officer / CFO (Strategic Commissioning Framework, finance)
talking_points: Your Net Zero Plan and six sectoral plans are exactly the kind of multi-year commitment the ANAO has flagged for governance scrutiny — independent gateway assurance and a commitment-tracking framework de-risk delivery and reporting.

The first full year of self-managed ICT is the highest-risk window for legacy migration and cyber exposure; we can provide build/implementation and PSPF/ISM assurance where insourcing can't scale fast enough.
CCE08 commits you to designing a National Adaptation Plan MEL system this year — we can deliver a PGPA-aligned evaluation framework tied to your Climate Risk Assessment priorities.
We understand the Strategic Commissioning Framework — our value is specialist capability, build/implementation and independent assurance, not generalist augmentation.
The 450 GL recovery target has a hard legislated deadline and heavy state dependencies; a delivery PMO with milestone assurance protects the 'all reasonable steps' position under the Water Act.
Your extensive performance-measure changes and reliance on qualitative/case-study measures are an evaluation-evidence risk we can strengthen ahead of annual reporting.
caveats: The plan is rich and clearly mapped, supporting high confidence on several opportunities (Net Zero assurance, ICT/cyber, adaptation MEL). However, the department's explicit commitment to insource core work under the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework materially constrains generalist advisory demand and should temper sizing of engagements — position around specialist, build and independent-assurance work. External research was incomplete due to a search-tool cap: no news/controversy items were verified, and ANAO audits were confirmed only by existence and subject (not findings), so those sections should be refreshed before relying on them. Opportunity confidence reflects plan evidence, not confirmed procurement intent.
